# PR Documentation Packet

Docs Forge owns the documentation content that appears on every PR.

## Required Body Section

Every PR body must contain:

```markdown
## Documentation

**Generated by:** oc-docs-forge
**Docs changed:** [README / product docs / ADR / changelog / API docs / none]
**Reader impact:** [What a reviewer or user needs to know]
**Follow-up docs:** [None / ticket ids / deferred PR comment marker]
```

If no docs changed, the section must still appear with a short reason.

## When To Update Repo Docs

Update README, product docs, ADRs, changelog, generated catalogs, or API docs when
the diff changes:

- User-facing behavior, commands, setup, install steps, env vars, screenshots, or
  workflows.
- Public API contracts, SDKs, schemas, model/tool contracts, or feature flags.
- Skill catalogs, command menus, governance metadata, checkpoint fields, or
  release surfaces.
- Operational playbooks, deployment steps, monitoring, incidents, or runbooks.

## PR Comment Marker

When the packet is too long for the PR body, post or prepare a PR comment:

```markdown
<!-- opchain:oc-docs-forge:pr-docs -->
# Documentation Packet
...
```

The PR body must link to or mention this comment marker.

## Verification

Verify against the current diff. A packet copied from an earlier commit is stale.
Block if files named in the packet do not exist or if the changed behavior is not
represented in docs or follow-up items.
